# Agent Behavior Enforcement — Refined Plan

## Context

The user-supplied proposal is sound in spirit (hooks-first, supervisor-as-async-auditor) but reads as greenfield. A repo audit shows roughly 70-80% of Layer 1 already ships, Layer 2 has explicit maintenance discipline, and Layer 3's deterministic half is the existing **Tier 3 compliance scorecard**. The plan below is the proposal, narrowed to the actual gap.

## Architectural framing (non-negotiable)

Per [`docs/foundation/layered_architecture.md`](docs/foundation/layered_architecture.md) and [`docs/foundation/philosophy.md`](docs/foundation/philosophy.md) §5.3 / MUST NOT 8/9/10:

- The auditor is an **operational-layer consumer**, not a substrate component. It reads observations and writes findings back through the same MCP/HTTP surfaces any other consumer uses. It MUST NOT live under `src/` (substrate scope) — it lives under `packages/` (operational scope).
- All auditor outputs are stored as Neotoma observations with full provenance (entity types: `instruction_diff_proposal`, `hook_suggestion`, `pattern_report`, plus existing `issue`). This dogfoods the substrate and gives every recommendation a traceable basis.
- The auditor MAY consume substrate signals via `subscribe` (per [`philosophy.md`](docs/foundation/philosophy.md) §5.9) — like any other operational subscriber. The substrate itself MUST NOT subscribe to its own signals.

This framing is what makes the auditor strengthen rather than weaken Neotoma's integrity story; the proposal's intuition is right but needs to be stated in the repo's own architectural vocabulary.

## What already exists (reframe of "Layer 1/2/3")

**Layer 1 (hooks)** — substantially built:
- `cursor-hooks` and `claude-code-plugin` implement: session-start MCP-instruction caching, prompt-submit retrieval injection + persistence safety net, post-tool-use tool-invocation capture + failure accumulator, pre-compact markers, stop-hook assistant safety net + compliance backfill + `followup_message` re-prompt (`loop_limit: 1`).
- `diagnoseSkippedStore()` in [`packages/cursor-hooks/hooks/_common.ts`](packages/cursor-hooks/hooks/_common.ts) classifies misses into five categories with `recommended_repairs[]` and `proactive_remediation_required` for in-repo runs.
- Schema rejection at write time is enforced via `ERR_STORE_RESOLUTION_FAILED` with structured `issues[].hint`.
- Failure-signal accumulator in [`packages/claude-code-plugin/hooks/_common.py`](packages/claude-code-plugin/hooks/_common.py) writes `tool_invocation_failure` entities and surfaces `submit_issue` hints; only the **agent decision to file** is manual today.

**Layer 2 (instructions)** — well-defined and disciplined:
- [`docs/developer/mcp/instructions.md`](docs/developer/mcp/instructions.md) is the source of truth, shipped at runtime by the MCP server. Sections `[TURN LIFECYCLE]`, `[STORE RECIPES]`, `[QA REFLECTION]`, `[ERRORS & RECOVERY]`, `[ISSUE REPORTING]` already encode the bulk of what the proposal calls "edge cases, examples, negative examples".
- Edit discipline lives in [`docs/developer/agent_instructions_sync_rules.mdc`](docs/developer/agent_instructions_sync_rules.mdc) and `architecture/change_guardrails_rules.mdc`. Adding new examples is a normal contribution flow, not new infrastructure.

**Layer 3 (auditor)** — deterministic half exists; probabilistic half does not:
- Tier 3 from [`docs/subsystems/agentic_eval.md`](docs/subsystems/agentic_eval.md) (services/compliance/*) reads `conversation_turn` observations, computes `backfill_rate` per `(model × harness × profile)` cell, and emits `issue` entities when thresholds are crossed. Triage flowchart already groups by `instruction_diagnostics.classification`.
- Tier 1 (hook-fixture replay) and Tier 2 (real-LLM replay with cassettes) provide the validation surface the proposal asks for ("run the auditor against historical data first").
- What is **missing** is a log-reading LLM auditor that proposes structured instruction diffs / hook suggestions / pattern reports beyond deterministic aggregation.

## Genuine gaps to close

1. **Pre-write retrieval gate (Layer 1).** No hook today blocks/warns when the agent calls `store` without first having issued a `retrieve_entity_by_identifier` / `retrieve_entities` call when the user message implied known entities. `[TURN LIFECYCLE]` step 1 is rule-enforced only post-hoc by the stop hook's classification, never pre-write.
2. **Auto-drafted issues from accumulated failure patterns (Layer 1).** The failure accumulator currently surfaces a one-line hint suggesting `submit_issue`; the agent decides whether to file. With `issues.reporting_mode = proactive`, the hook itself could draft and submit an `issue` entity with `submit_issue` from accumulated `tool_invocation_failure` patterns at session end, gated by `consent`/`off` modes that already exist.
3. **Log-reading LLM auditor (Layer 3).** New package `packages/neotoma-auditor/` (operational layer). Reads `conversation_turn` rows + recent `tool_invocation_failure` clusters, runs an LLM pass, emits structured outputs as Neotoma observations. Outputs are reviewed by a human (or another operator-authority agent) before any change is merged into instructions or hooks. Wires into Tier 3's existing alert-check cron entry point.

## Concrete deliverables

- **Replace the proposal in this thread with a refined doc** at `docs/private/strategy/agent_behavior_enforcement.md`. The doc cites the existing pieces above and scopes only the three gaps. Move the "Layer 1/2/3" framing into a "What exists / What is missing" framing so future readers don't re-litigate the architecture.
- **New PreToolUse hook surface** in [`packages/cursor-hooks/hooks/`](packages/cursor-hooks/hooks/) and [`packages/claude-code-plugin/hooks/`](packages/claude-code-plugin/hooks/): `pre_tool_use.{ts,py}`. Inspects the pending tool call; if it is a Neotoma `store` for non-bookkeeping entities and the per-turn state shows `retrieve_calls === 0` against an identifier-bearing prompt, returns a structured warning (or block, behind `NEOTOMA_HOOK_RETRIEVAL_GATE=block`). Default `warn` to avoid breaking established sessions.
- **Auto-issue-drafting in stop / failure path.** Extend [`_common.py`](packages/claude-code-plugin/hooks/_common.py) and [`_common.ts`](packages/cursor-hooks/hooks/_common.ts): when accumulated `tool_invocation_failure` for `(tool, error_class)` crosses a `NEOTOMA_HOOK_AUTO_ISSUE_THRESHOLD` (default 3) within a session AND `issues.reporting_mode === "proactive"`, call `submit_issue` directly with PII-redacted body. Reuses existing redaction (`scrub_error_message`, `classify_error_message`).
- **`packages/neotoma-auditor/`** (new operational package, mirrors `packages/eval-harness/` layout):
  - CLI: `neotoma-auditor run --since 7d --output-mode store|stdout|both`.
  - Reads `conversation_turn` (filterable by `instruction_diagnostics.classification`), `tool_invocation_failure`, recent `issue` rows.
  - Prompts an LLM with the canonical instruction text and a redacted sample of failures; produces structured JSON: `{ instruction_diff_proposals[], hook_suggestions[], pattern_reports[], issue_drafts[] }`.
  - Persists outputs as Neotoma entities via `store`, with `data_source: "neotoma-auditor"` and provenance pointing to the source `conversation_turn` rows.
  - Replay mode reuses Tier 1 / Tier 2 fixtures so the auditor can be evaluated before wiring to live data.
- **Schema registrations** for new entity types via `update_schema_incremental`: `instruction_diff_proposal` (fields: `target_doc_path`, `before_excerpt`, `after_excerpt`, `evidence_turn_ids[]`, `confidence`), `hook_suggestion` (fields: `hook_event`, `proposed_behavior`, `evidence_turn_ids[]`), `pattern_report` (fields: `pattern_name`, `summary`, `cell_aggregates`, `window_start`, `window_end`).
- **Inspector surface**: a small page at `/inspector/auditor` that lists pending `instruction_diff_proposal` / `hook_suggestion` / `pattern_report` entities for human review, with accept/reject affordances. Mirrors how `/inspector/compliance` already surfaces Tier 3 data.
- **MCP instructions update**: a new short `[AUDITOR LOOP]` section in [`docs/developer/mcp/instructions.md`](docs/developer/mcp/instructions.md) describing how operators wire the auditor and how its outputs are reviewed. Enforced via `agent_instructions_sync_rules.mdc`.

## Sequencing

1. **Audit current hooks against observed failures** (existing Tier 1 fixtures + production `conversation_turn` rows). Map each observed failure mode to: (a) existing hook that fires, (b) advisory that should be blocking, (c) genuinely missing primitive. Output: a one-page gap matrix added to the refined doc.
2. **Pre-write retrieval gate** (warn-mode default), with new Tier 1 fixture (`agent_stores_without_retrieval`) and Tier 2 scenario.
3. **Auto-issue-drafting in proactive mode**, with Tier 1 fixture covering `(tool_invocation_failure threshold, reporting_mode=proactive)`.
4. **Build `packages/neotoma-auditor/`** in offline / replay-only mode against historical fixtures. No live wiring yet.
5. **Validate auditor outputs** against the existing `agent_skips_store_after_reminder`, `tool_failure_recovery`, and other Tier 1 fixtures plus a curated sample of real `conversation_turn` rows. Human-review every output before it changes a hook or instruction.
6. **Wire auditor into Tier 3 alert-check cron** (operator opt-in via `NEOTOMA_AUDITOR_ENABLED=1`). Outputs flow through Inspector review queue.
7. **Migrate auditor findings into hooks and instructions over time.** Track in `pattern_report` entities so the migration itself is auditable.

## Constraints to hold the line on

- **Auditor never mutates live agent state.** Outputs are observations; humans (or an explicitly authorized operator agent) decide.
- **Auditor never lives under `src/`.** Substrate-scope rules in [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) and [`docs/foundation/core_identity.md`](docs/foundation/core_identity.md) forbid strategy/inference logic in the state layer.
- **Hooks stay deterministic.** Anything probabilistic moves to the auditor package.
- **No parallel eval framework.** Auditor evaluation reuses Tier 1 fixtures and Tier 2 cassettes; a new framework would be the first sign of architectural drift.
- **No real-time supervisor.** The proposal's prohibition is correct and stays in force; this plan codifies it.

## Open questions to resolve before commit

- **Retrieval-gate severity default**: warn or block? Recommend `warn` for v1 with `NEOTOMA_HOOK_RETRIEVAL_GATE=block` opt-in; revisit after one cycle of Tier 1/Tier 2 evaluation. Asking before scoping.
- **Auditor invocation cadence**: scheduled (cron, like Tier 3 alerts), event-driven (subscribe to `turn_complete` substrate signals), or on-demand only? Recommend on-demand + scheduled for v1; event-driven once the substrate signaling primitive is GA.
- **Review surface for auditor outputs**: Inspector queue (operator-friendly) vs. PRs against `docs/developer/mcp/instructions.md` (engineering-friendly)? Recommend Inspector queue with one-click "open as PR" affordance for accepted instruction diffs.
- **Should `issues.reporting_mode = proactive` automatically extend to hook-driven auto-drafts**, or require a separate `hooks.auto_issue_mode`? Recommend reusing the existing mode for consistency; flag if separate gating is required for noise control.
